af_score_components.notes: Local MCP server bridging LSP (Language Server Protocol) to AI clients (quality 70). No auth required — local operation. Beta software — APIs may change, edge cases exist. Language server binary (gopls, rust-analyzer, pyright, etc.) must be installed separately and on PATH before starting. Only stdio-compatible LSP servers supported — network-based LSP needs a wrapper. Language server startup (especially rust-analyzer on large projects) causes initial latency. Workspace path must be correctly specified in MCP config. Provides semantic navigation: go-to-definition, find-references, diagnostics, hover docs, rename symbol. Excellent for AI-assisted refactoring and large codebase navigation.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local tool — no network exposure. No credentials required or stored. Read-only semantic analysis operations (rename is write but workspace-scoped). Community maintained beta software — dependency hygiene depends on maintainer. Language server binary from trusted sources (gopls, rust-analyzer, pyright) have good security track records. Workspace path scoping provides implicit access boundary. No compliance certifications. macOS/Linux local access controls provide implicit security boundary. Beta status means potential for security edge cases in LSP compatibility.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted local tool — no SLA. Beta software — APIs may change without notice. No formal breaking change policy. Language server startup time (rust-analyzer: 30+ seconds on large Rust projects) creates initial latency. Partial idempotency — read operations idempotent, rename has side effects. No retry guidance. LSP compatibility varies across language server implementations — edge cases documented. Language server binary must be on PATH — PATH issues are a common failure mode. Not suitable for production unattended deployments.

af_score_components.notes: Go MCP server that auto-generates MCP tools from any OpenAPI V3 specification (quality 68). Auth via 'h=' header parameter at startup (static JSON headers — API key or bearer token). OAuth interactive flows explicitly NOT supported — a key limitation. Path filtering via wildcard expressions to expose subset of endpoints. Cursor pagination. OpenAPI V2/Swagger requires conversion to V3 first. Limited MIME type support may break file upload or binary response endpoints. Resource-based interaction noted as a current limitation. No pre-built binaries — requires Go build environment. Excellent rapid prototyping tool for OpenAPI-spec'd APIs.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for target API connections. Static header-based auth — API key or bearer token via 'h=' parameter at startup. No OAuth flow support means tokens must be manually rotated. Path filtering can expose unintended endpoints if misconfigured — review wildcard patterns carefully. Community Go project from automation-ai-labs. Auth headers passed at startup — visible in process arguments (not env vars). No scope control beyond path filtering. Target API scoping depends on the API's own token permissions. No compliance certifications.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — no SLA. Community Go project with no formal versioning. No formal breaking change policy. Depends on target API uptime and OpenAPI spec accuracy. No retry guidance. Partial idempotency — GET endpoints idempotent, mutation endpoints are not. MIME type limitations may cause silent failures on binary endpoints. Static auth headers expire without auto-refresh. OpenAPI spec changes require server restart to pick up new endpoint definitions. Cursor pagination supported.

af_score_components.notes: Persistent memory backend MCP server with hybrid BM25 + vector search (quality 78). Three auth modes: anonymous (MCP_ALLOW_ANONYMOUS_ACCESS=true for local dev), API key (MCP_API_KEY for production), OAuth2.1 (MCP_OAUTH_ENABLED for team collaboration). Python 3.13 incompatible — use Python 3.12 (sqlite-vec issue). macOS system Python lacks sqlite extensions — must use Homebrew Python. MCP_MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS must be tuned to prevent large memory retrievals from overflowing agent context. X-Agent-ID header auto-tagging for multi-agent identity management. Cloudflare sync for multi-device/multi-agent sharing requires Cloudflare account. Windows Claude Code users need /session-start hook workaround.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for HTTP dashboard and Cloudflare sync. Three auth modes: anonymous is explicitly for local dev only — disable in production. API key via environment variable for production. OAuth2.1 for team collaboration. X-Agent-ID tagging for multi-agent isolation — must be managed carefully. Cloudflare Workers KV for sync requires Cloudflare API credentials. Community maintained Python project — Python 3.13 incompatibility shows active but careful maintenance. No compliance certifications. Memory may contain sensitive context — access control critical for team deployments.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted local-first with optional Cloudflare sync — no SLA. Active development with documented Python version constraints. No formal breaking change policy. MCP_MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS tuning required to prevent context overflow — operational gotcha. Cloudflare hybrid backend availability is a separate reliability concern. sqlite-vec dependency has Python version constraints. Partial idempotency — memory retrieval idempotent, memory storage may have duplicates. No retry guidance. Windows SessionStart hook workaround indicates cross-platform reliability concerns.

af_score_components.notes: Local MCP server for Mermaid diagram generation (quality 70). No auth required. Three transport protocols: stdio, SSE on port 3033, Streamable on port 1122 — correct config required for client. mermaid.ink public service for shareable URLs (no SLA — may fail silently under high load). Local file PNG export writes to filesystem — agent must have write permissions. Full idempotency for diagram generation. Supports SVG, PNG, base64, shareable URL export formats. Configurable themes and backgrounds. Very large or deeply nested Mermaid diagrams may fail to render. Good for AI-driven documentation and architecture diagram workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: mermaid.ink uses HTTPS for URL-based exports. No credentials required or stored. Local filesystem write for PNG export — file permissions controlled by OS. Community maintained npm package. mermaid.ink is a public service — diagram content sent to public service via URL encoding; avoid sensitive data in diagrams. No compliance certifications. Diagram syntax validation prevents some injection vectors. No network exposure for local stdio transport. Simple, low-risk security profile.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted with mermaid.ink as optional public service (no documented SLA). Community maintained npm. No formal breaking change policy. Full idempotency — same Mermaid syntax always produces same diagram. mermaid.ink may fail silently under high load — use local rendering for reliability. Mermaid syntax complexity limits apply — very large diagrams may fail. Three transport protocols require correct client configuration. File write requires filesystem permissions. No retry guidance.

af_score_components.notes: Suite of four Neo4j Labs MCP servers (quality 70): Cypher query execution, persistent knowledge graph memory, Aura cloud management, and interactive data modeling. EXPERIMENTAL Labs project — breaking changes possible without notice. APOC plugin must be installed separately for schema introspection. Natural language to Cypher translation can produce incorrect queries on complex schemas. Username/password for Cypher/memory servers; Aura API client ID/secret for cloud management. Not suitable for production workloads. No retry guidance. Good for AI-assisted graph database exploration and knowledge graph workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for Aura cloud management. TLS for Neo4j database connections configurable. Username/password for local Neo4j; Aura client ID/secret for cloud. No fine-grained MCP-level scope control — database user permissions control access. Neo4j Labs experimental project — less rigorous security review than official Neo4j products. Credentials via environment variables. Natural language Cypher generation could produce unintended write operations — review generated queries. No compliance certifications for Labs projects. APOC plugin adds dependency surface.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Labs experimental — breaking changes possible without notice. No SLA on MCP server. Neo4j Aura has SLA for cloud instances; self-hosted Neo4j has no SLA. APOC plugin required for schema — missing plugin breaks schema tools. Partial idempotency — Cypher reads idempotent, writes are not. Natural language to Cypher translation unreliable for complex schemas — verify generated queries. No retry guidance. Aura management operations (create/delete instances) have production financial impact. Not suitable for production agent workflows.

af_score_components.notes: Chrome extension + local MCP server for DOM inspection (quality 65). No auth required — local WebSocket on port 7007. Human-in-the-loop REQUIRED — developer must hold Option/Alt and click to provide DOM context (not automatable). Chrome/Chromium-based only — Firefox not supported. React component detection is experimental and may not work on all React versions. Port 7007 must be free with no alternate port configuration. Chrome extension must be installed and active. Excellent for interactive web development with AI coding assistants needing precise DOM context. Not suitable for automated/headless workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local WebSocket — no network exposure. No credentials required or stored. Chrome extension requires installation in user browser — extension trust model applies. Human-in-the-loop (Option/Alt click) is an implicit security mechanism — no unauthorized data extraction without user action. DOM data captured includes computed styles and attributes — may include sensitive page content. No credentials stored locally. Community maintained. No compliance certifications. Port 7007 local binding provides implicit isolation.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted local tool — no SLA. Chrome extension + MCP server must both be active. React detection experimental — may not work on all React versions. Idempotency: yes — DOM inspection is read-only and deterministic. Port 7007 must be free — no alternate port configuration documented. No retry guidance. Human-in-the-loop requirement prevents automation. Chrome extension updates may break compatibility with local MCP server. Not suitable for automated regression testing pipelines.

af_score_components.notes: Bidirectional stdio/SSE/Streamable HTTP MCP transport bridge (quality 75). Bearer token via API_ACCESS_TOKEN env var, custom headers via --headers flag, and OAuth2 flow. Stateless mode for Streamable HTTP may cause issues with stateful servers. SSL verification may be disabled in some configurations — verify settings. CORS must be explicitly configured via --allow-origin. OAuth2 token refresh behavior under long-running agent sessions not clearly documented. Adds latency and a failure point as proxy layer. Good for bridging Claude Desktop (stdio-only) to remote HTTP/SSE MCP servers.

security_score_components.security_notes: SSL verification can be disabled — verify this is not set in security-sensitive environments. Bearer token via API_ACCESS_TOKEN env var (good). --headers flag for custom auth may expose secrets in process argument lists (bad). CORS must be explicitly configured — open CORS is a security risk. OAuth2 support is good but long-session token refresh is undocumented. Community Python project. No scope control at proxy level. Auth passes through to underlying MCP servers. No compliance certifications. Proxy adds network surface area.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — no SLA. Community maintained Python project. No formal breaking change policy. Stateless mode may break stateful MCP servers — test compatibility. Unknown idempotency — depends on proxied server. OAuth2 long-session token refresh behavior undocumented — may fail for long-running agents. Adds latency as additional proxy hop. No retry guidance. CORS misconfiguration can cause silent failures. Additional failure point vs direct client-server connection.

af_score_components.notes: Official Redis MCP server for natural language Redis access (quality 80). Auth: username/password + TLS, or Azure Entra ID with managed identity and automatic token renewal. Redis ACL-based role restrictions supported. Requires Python 3.14+ — very recent requirement that may break existing environments. HTTP streaming transport planned but not yet available — stdio only. Vector index management requires Redis Stack or Redis with Search module. Cursor pagination. Azure EntraID token renewal automatic but test edge cases for long-running agents. Good for caching, session state, and vector search in agent workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS supported and recommended for production Redis connections. Azure Entra ID with managed identity and DefaultAzureCredential — excellent cloud-native auth. Redis ACL-based role restrictions enable per-command and per-key scoping. Official Redis project — rigorous security review. Credentials via environment variables; Azure managed identity avoids static credential management. Python 3.14+ very recent requirement may introduce new dependency risks. Redis ACL configuration is responsibility of Redis deployment. Vector index access control inherits Redis ACL.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on Redis instance uptime — Redis Cloud/Redis Enterprise have SLAs. Official Redis project with good change management. Cursor pagination for large result sets. No retry guidance documented. Partial idempotency — reads idempotent, writes may have side effects. Python 3.14+ requirement is very new — may have compatibility issues with existing Python environments. HTTP streaming transport not yet available — stdio only limits deployment flexibility. Azure token renewal automatic but long-running session edge cases should be tested. Vector search requires Redis Stack module.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server for AI GUI control over remote macOS via Apple Screen Sharing (VNC) (quality 55). macOS username/password via Docker env vars (MACOS_USERNAME, MACOS_PASSWORD) — stored as plain-text environment variables. Only Apple Authentication (Diffie-Hellman, protocol 30) supported — incompatible with some VNC clients. Screen capture + LLM vision round-trips create high latency per action. No built-in completion detection for actions (app finished loading, etc.). Fragile to UI layout changes, retina scaling, dark mode. No idempotency — each action is a one-way GUI interaction. Useful for automating macOS-specific apps without API. VPN strongly recommended for remote access.

security_score_components.security_notes: CRITICAL: macOS credentials (username/password) stored as plain-text Docker environment variables — significant risk in shared or CI/CD environments. Apple Screen Sharing (VNC-based) with DH auth (protocol 30) — older protocol without modern TLS security. Full macOS GUI access — no scope control whatsoever; anything visible on screen is accessible. Community maintained. No compliance certifications. VPN strongly recommended for non-local use. Binding to 0.0.0.0 exposes credentials and GUI access to network. Security score of 45 reflects the plain-text credential storage and full-access scope model.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — no SLA. Community maintained. No formal breaking change policy. No idempotency — GUI actions are one-way and state-dependent. Screen capture + LLM vision round-trips are slow (seconds per action). No completion detection — agents may act before previous action finishes. Fragile to UI changes: retina scaling, dark mode, app updates all affect reliability. Protocol 30 (Apple DH) only — incompatible with some VNC server configurations. Docker-based deployment simplifies setup but adds container overhead. Not suitable for high-frequency or time-sensitive automation.

af_score_components.notes: Desktop GUI application for centralized MCP server management (quality 68). Token-based auth for custom app connections — locally issued, all data remains local. Workspace grouping for organizing MCP servers by project context. Individual tool enable/disable without removing server. Integrated request logging for audit. Works with Claude, Cline, Cursor, Windsurf. GUI-first — configuration changes require manual interaction (not programmable). Multi-user or team sharing not supported — each install is personal. Token rotation/expiry process not documented. Good for developers managing 10+ MCP servers who want organization without editing JSON config files.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local desktop app — no network exposure by design. All data remains local — no external auth service. Token-based auth for custom app connections. Workspace-level grouping provides logical isolation. Tool enable/disable provides coarse scope control. Community maintained desktop app — unknown dependency hygiene. No compliance certifications. Local access controls provide implicit security boundary. Token rotation and expiry process undocumented — rotation required periodically. Integrated request logging may capture sensitive tool inputs/outputs — review retention.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Desktop application — no SLA. Requires desktop environment (not headless). Community maintained with no formal versioning. No formal breaking change policy. GUI-first — configuration changes require manual interaction (not scripted). Unknown idempotency for MCP server management operations. Token management process undocumented. No retry guidance. Single-user personal tool — not suitable for team or shared deployments. Works across Claude, Cline, Cursor, Windsurf — broad compatibility. No retry guidance.
